req.title: Manager deletes training-course
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a training-course through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers authenticated manager deletion of a training-course via UI. I derive: (1) a positive EP/STC case – valid manager deletes an existing course, verifying 200/201, entity removal, and audit record; (2) a negative NEG case – unauthenticated user receives 401; (3) a negative DT case – forbidden role (employee) receives 403; (4) a boundary BVA case – deleting the last remaining training-course (edge of the data set, state boundary); (5) a negative NEG case – attempting to delete an already-deleted course returns 400 or 404 (invalid state). The violation in test[4] was kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC is reserved for positive state-transition coverage. Fixed by changing heuristic to NEG for the already-deleted scenario, which is a negative/error-state test.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_existing_training_course_success
preconditions: At least one training-course exists with known id
actions: Locate the target course row by id
Click the Delete button for that course
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Assert UI response and audit record
expected: Response body contains deleted entity id
Training-course no longer appears in the course list
Audit record for the deletion is written
trace: R-WEB-047: positive path – manager deletes course
R-WEB-047: audit record must be written on success
R-WEB-047: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_existing_training_course_success(
    manager_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for manager
    create_training_course, # <fixture>: creates a course, returns {id, title}
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: client to query audit records
    api_client_manager,     # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for manager
):
    course = create_training_course({"title": "Safety Basics", "duration": 60})
    course_id = course["id"]

    manager_page.goto("/training-courses")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-course-id='{course_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()
    row.locator("button[data-action='delete']").click()
    manager_page.locator("button[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    resp = api_client_manager.get(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201, 404), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"

    expect(manager_page.locator(f"[data-course-id='{course_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        entity_type="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written for deletion"
    assert audit_entries[0]["entity_id"] == course_id

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_training_course
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no session cookie / token)
At least one training-course exists with known id
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/training-courses/{id} without auth headers
expected: Response body contains authentication error message
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_training_course(
    create_training_course, # <fixture>: creates course as manager, returns {id}
    anon_api_client,        # <fixture>: unauthenticated requests.Session
    api_client_manager,     # <fixture>: authenticated session to verify record intact
):
    course = create_training_course({"title": "Fire Safety", "duration": 45})
    course_id = course["id"]

    resp = anon_api_client.delete(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

    verify = api_client_manager.get(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Course should still exist after failed delete"

tests.name: test_employee_role_forbidden_to_delete_training_course
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (not manager)
At least one training-course exists with known id
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/training-courses/{id} with employee auth token
expected: Response body indicates forbidden / insufficient role
trace: R-WEB-047: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-047: actor must be manager (decision: role != manager => deny)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_employee_role_forbidden_to_delete_training_course(
    create_training_course,  # <fixture>: creates course as manager, returns {id}
    api_client_employee,     # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for employee role
    api_client_manager,      # <fixture>: authenticated session to verify record intact
):
    course = create_training_course({"title": "GDPR Awareness", "duration": 30})
    course_id = course["id"]

    resp = api_client_employee.delete(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert "forbidden" in body.get("message", "").lower() or \
           "not authorized" in body.get("message", "").lower(), \
           f"Unexpected error message: {body}"

    verify = api_client_manager.get(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Course should still exist after forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_last_remaining_training_course
preconditions: Exactly ONE training-course exists in the system (boundary: count = 1)
actions: Verify exactly one course is listed
Click Delete on the sole course
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Assert UI empty state and audit record
expected: Audit record for the deletion is written
trace: R-WEB-047: boundary – deleting the last record (count transitions 1 -> 0)
R-WEB-047: audit record must be written on success
BVA: list size at lower boundary after delete
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_last_remaining_training_course(
    manager_page,
    ensure_single_training_course, # <fixture>: removes all courses then creates 1, returns {id}
    audit_log_client,
):
    course = ensure_single_training_course({"title": "Lone Course", "duration": 90})
    course_id = course["id"]

    manager_page.goto("/training-courses")
    rows = manager_page.locator("[data-course-id]")
    expect(rows).to_have_count(1)

    rows.first.locator("button[data-action='delete']").click()
    manager_page.locator("button[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-testid='empty-state']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-course-id]")).to_have_count(0)

    entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        entity_type="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE"
    )
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Audit record missing for last-course deletion"

tests.name: test_manager_cannot_delete_already_deleted_training_course
preconditions: A training-course has already been deleted (id is known but resource is gone)
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/training-courses/{id} for an already-deleted course id
Assert response status and error body
expected: Response body contains an appropriate error message indicating resource not found or invalid state
No duplicate audit records are written
trace: R-WEB-047: error pathway – 400 on validation error (invalid/non-existent resource id)
NEG: negative state – resource no longer exists, re-deletion must fail gracefully
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_cannot_delete_already_deleted_training_course(
    create_training_course,  # <fixture>: creates course as manager, returns {id}
    api_client_manager,      # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for manager
    audit_log_client,        # <fixture>: client to query audit records
):
    course = create_training_course({"title": "Expired Course", "duration": 20})
    course_id = course["id"]

    # First deletion – should succeed
    first_resp = api_client_manager.delete(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert first_resp.status_code in (200, 201, 204), \
        f"Initial delete failed unexpectedly: {first_resp.status_code}"

    # Second deletion – should fail
    second_resp = api_client_manager.delete(f"/api/training-courses/{course_id}")
    assert second_resp.status_code in (400, 404), \
        f"Expected 400 or 404 on re-delete, got {second_resp.status_code}"

    body = second_resp.json()
    assert body.get("message") or body.get("error"), \
        "Response body should contain an error message"

    # Verify no extra audit entry was created by the second attempt
    entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        entity_type="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE"
    )
    assert len(entries) == 1, \
        f"Expected exactly 1 audit entry, found {len(entries)}"
